<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <reaction/concurrency/thread_manager.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 数据模型定义
// ============================================

class TestObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Create observer relationship and test notification
 *
 * @param observerName Name for the observer node
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return std::pair<bool, int> Pair of whether observer was notified and call count
 */
std::pair<bool, int> test_observer_notification(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    TestObserver observer(observerName);
    TestObserver target(targetName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship (observer observes target)
    observerGraph.addObserver(target, observer);
    
    // 4. Notify target of change
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    // 5. Return notification status
    return {observer.wasChanged(), observer.getCallCount()};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// 函数实现
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test atomic operations on reactive variables
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable
 * @param addValue Value to add atomically
 * @return int The final value after atomic addition
 */
int test_atomic_operations(int initialValue, int addValue) {
    // Create a reactive variable using reaction::var with the initial value
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // Use the += operator to perform atomic addition
    reactiveVar += addValue;
    
    // Use the get() method to retrieve the current value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // Return the final value
    return finalValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestObserverReset : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestObserverReset(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false), m_callCount(0) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**

 *

 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @return bool Whether observer reset worked correctly (observer no longer notified after reset)
 */
bool test_observer_reset(const std::string& observerName, const std::string& targetName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto target = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    auto observer = std::make_shared<TestObserverReset>(observerName);
    
    // Set names for debugging/tracing
    target->setName(targetName);
    observer->setName(observerName);
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Add observer relationship
    graph.addObserver(target, observer);

    target->valueChanged(true);

        return false; // Test failed: observer wasn't notified initially

    // 8. Verify observer was not notified after reset
    if (observer->wasChanged()) {
        return false; // Test failed: observer was notified after reset
    }
    
    // Additional verification: call count should remain the same
    if (observer->getCallCount() != callCountBeforeReset) {
        return false; // Test failed: call count changed unexpectedly
    }
    
    // 9. Return success status
    return true;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <stdexcept>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestTransactionalObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestTransactionalObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test transactional observer updates
 *
 * @param nodeName Name for the node to update
 * @return bool Whether transactional update succeeded (no cycle created)
 */
bool test_transactional_update(const std::string& nodeName) {
    // 1. Create observer nodes
    auto source = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("source");
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestTransactionalObserver>("target");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Set up initial relationship
    observerGraph.addObserver(source, target);
    
    // 4. Notify target and verify relationship works
    source->valueChanged(true);
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Relationship verification failed
    }
    
    // 5. Reset observer for next test
    target->reset();
    
    // 6. Attempt transactional update that would create cycle
    bool cycleExceptionCaught = false;
    
    try {
        // Attempt to create cycle: target observes source (already observes target)
        observerGraph.addObserver(target, source);
        
        // If we reach here, no exception was thrown
        cycleExceptionCaught = false;
    }
    catch (const std::exception& e) {
        // 7. Cycle exception caught
        cycleExceptionCaught = true;
        
        // Verify the exception message contains cycle information
        if (std::string(e.what()).find("cycle") == std::string::npos) {
            return false; // Wrong exception type caught
        }
    }
    
    // 8. Notify target again
    source->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 9. Verify original relationship is preserved
    if (!target->wasChanged() || target->getCallCount() != 1) {
        return false; // Original relationship not preserved
    }
    
    // 10. Return success status
    return cycleExceptionCaught;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestMultipleObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestMultipleObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test multiple observers watching a single target
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observerNames Vector of names for observer nodes
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether all observers were notified
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_multiple_observers(const std::string& targetName, const std::vector<std::string>& observerNames) {
    // 1. Create target node
    TestMultipleObserver target(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create observer nodes
    std::vector<TestMultipleObserver> observers;
    for (const auto& name : observerNames) {
        observers.emplace_back(name);
    }
    
    // 3. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 4. Add all observers to watch target
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        graph.addObserver(target, observer);
    }
    
    // 5. Notify target
    target.valueChanged(true);
    
    // 6. Count notifications and verify all observers were notified
    int totalCallCount = 0;
    bool allNotified = true;
    
    for (auto& observer : observers) {
        totalCallCount += observer.getCallCount();
        if (observer.getCallCount() == 0) {
            allNotified = false;
        }
    }
    
    // 7. Return total call count and notification status
    return {totalCallCount, allNotified};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Data model definitions
// ============================================

class TestChainedObserver : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    TestChainedObserver(const std::string& name) : m_name(name), m_changed(false) {}

    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_changed = changed;
        m_callCount++;
        // Call base class to propagate notification to observers
        ObserverNode::valueChanged(changed);
    }

    bool wasChanged() const { return m_changed; }
    int getCallCount() const { return m_callCount; }
    const std::string& getName() const { return m_name; }

    void reset() {
        m_changed = false;
        m_callCount = 0;
    }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    bool m_changed;
    int m_callCount = 0;
};

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test chained observer relationships
 *
 * @param targetName Name for the target node
 * @param observer1Name Name for the first observer node
 * @param observer2Name Name for the second observer node (observes observer1)
 * @return std::pair<int, bool> Pair of total call count and whether chain notification worked correctly
 */
std::pair<int, bool> test_chained_observers(const std::string& targetName, 
                                            const std::string& observer1Name, 
                                            const std::string& observer2Name) {
    // 1. Create target node
    auto target = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(targetName);
    
    // 2. Create first observer node
    auto observer1 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer1Name);
    
    // 3. Create second observer node
    auto observer2 = std::make_shared<TestChainedObserver>(observer2Name);
    
    // 4. Get observer graph instance
    auto& graph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Add target as observer to observer1 (observer1 observes target)
    graph.addObserver(observer1, target);
    
    // 6. Add observer1 as observer to observer2 (observer2 observes observer1)
    graph.addObserver(observer2, observer1);
    
    // 7. Reset all change flags
    target->reset();
    observer1->reset();
    observer2->reset();
    
    // 8. Notify target
    target->valueChanged(true);
    
    // 9. Check if observer1 was notified
    bool observer1Notified = observer1->wasChanged();
    
    // 10. Check if observer2 was notified  
    bool observer2Notified = observer2->wasChanged();
    
    // 11. Calculate total call count
    int totalCalls = observer1->getCallCount() + observer2->getCallCount();
    
    // 12. Chain status: both observers must have been notified
    bool chainStatus = observer1Notified && observer2Notified;
    
    // 13. Return pair with total calls and chain status
    return std::make_pair(totalCalls, chainStatus);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test reactive action with side effects
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value for the reactive variable

int test_reactive_action(int initialValue, int newValue) {

    return capturedValue;
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

Please use functions from the `reaction` library to implement the `test_batch_execution` function.

The function should:
1. Create reactive variables
2. Create variable to track recomputation count
3. Create calculation
4. Perform non-batch updates
5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
6. Create batch context
7. Execute batch
8. Record recompute count after batch updates
9. Return final sum and counts

Please output the complete contents of the file:

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test batch execution reduces recomputation count
 *
 * @param initialA Initial value for variable a
 * @param initialB Initial value for variable b
 * @param nonBatchA Value to set a without batching
 * @param nonBatchB Value to set b without batching
 * @param batchA Value to set a within batch
 * @param batchB Value to set b within batch
 * @return std::tuple<int, int, int> {final sum, count after non-batch updates, count after batch updates}
 */
std::tuple<int, int, int> test_batch_execution(int initialA,
                                               int initialB,
                                               int nonBatchA,
                                               int nonBatchB,
                                               int batchA,
                                               int batchB) {
    // 1. Create reactive variables
    auto a = reaction::var(initialA);
    auto b = reaction::var(initialB);
    
    // 2. Create variable to track recomputation count
    auto recompCount = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that sums a and b and increments count
    auto sumCalc = reaction::calc([&recompCount, &a, &b]() {
        recompCount.value(recompCount.get() + 1);
        return a.get() + b.get();
    });
    
    // 4. Perform non-batch updates
    a.value(nonBatchA);
    b.value(nonBatchB);
    
    // 5. Record recompute count after non-batch updates
    int countAfterNonBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 6. Create batch context and execute batch
    reaction::batch([&]() {
        a.value(batchA);
        b.value(batchB);
    });
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after batch updates
    int countAfterBatch = recompCount.get();
    
    // 9. Return final sum and counts
    return {sumCalc.get(), countAfterNonBatch, countAfterBatch};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <tuple>
#include <cmath>

namespace test_reaction {

// ============================================
// Function implementations
// ============================================

/**
 * Function: Test filter trigger with conditional recomputation
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial sensor value
 * @param firstUpdate First update (expected to be within threshold)
 * @param secondUpdate Second update (expected to exceed threshold)
 * @param diffThreshold Minimum delta to trigger recomputation
 * @param levelThreshold Threshold to categorize HIGH vs NORMAL
 * @return std::tuple<std::string, int, int>
 *         {final status string, recompute count after first update, recompute count after second update}
 */
std::tuple<std::string, int, int> test_filter_trigger(double initialValue,
                                                      double firstUpdate,
                                                      double secondUpdate,
                                                      double diffThreshold,
                                                      double levelThreshold) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable for sensor
    auto sensor = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create variables to track recomputation count and last processed value
    int recomputeCount = 0;
    double lastProcessedValue = initialValue;
    
    // 3. Create filter-triggered calculation
    auto statusCalc = reaction::calc<reaction::FilterTrig>([&]() -> std::string {
        double current = sensor();
        
        // 4. Apply filter logic
        if (std::abs(current - lastProcessedValue) > diffThreshold) {
            lastProcessedValue = current;
            recomputeCount++;
        }
        
        // Categorize status based on level threshold
        if (current > levelThreshold) {
            return "HIGH";
        } else {
            return "NORMAL";
        }
    });
    
    // 5. Update sensor with first value
    sensor.value(firstUpdate);
    
    // 6. Record recompute count after first update
    int countAfterFirst = recomputeCount;
    
    // 7. Update sensor with second value
    sensor.value(secondUpdate);
    
    // 8. Record recompute count after second update
    int countAfterSecond = recomputeCount;
    
    // 9. Get final status
    std::string finalStatus = statusCalc();
    
    // 10. Return final status and counts
    return std::make_tuple(finalStatus, countAfterFirst, countAfterSecond);
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class CacheProbeNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override;
};

struct CacheStatsSnapshot {
    size_t graphEntriesBeforeCleanup;
    size_t graphEntriesAfterCleanup;
    double graphHitRatioAfterCleanup;
};

CacheStatsSnapshot test_observer_cache_stats() {
    // 1. Create CacheProbeNode instances
    auto nodeA = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeB = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    auto nodeC = std::make_shared<CacheProbeNode>();
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto& observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeA);
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeB);
    observerGraph.registerNode(nodeC);
    
    // 4. Set up direct observer relationship
    nodeA->addOneObserver(nodeB);
    
    // 5. Set up transitive observer relationship
    nodeB->addOneObserver(nodeC);
    
    // 6. Create NodeSet to store collected observers
    reaction::NodeSet observers;
    
    // 7. Populate cache data by traversing the graph
    nodeA->getObservers(observers);
    nodeB->getObservers(observers);
    
    // 8. Get cache statistics before cleanup
    auto cacheStatsBefore = observerGraph.getCacheStats();
    
    // 9. Trigger cache cleanup
    observerGraph.triggerCacheCleanup();
    
    // 10. Get cache statistics after cleanup
    auto cacheStatsAfter = observerGraph.getCacheStats();
    
    // 11. Close all nodes
    nodeA->close();
    nodeB->close();
    nodeC->close();
    
    // 12. Return cache stats snapshot
    return CacheStatsSnapshot{
        cacheStatsBefore.totalEntries,
        cacheStatsAfter.totalEntries,
        cacheStatsAfter.hitRatio
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

**Explanation:**

1. **Node Creation**: Creates three `CacheProbeNode` instances to establish both direct and transitive observer relationships.

2. **Graph Access**: Retrieves the singleton `ObserverGraph` instance to manage node relationships.

3. **Node Registration**: Registers each node with the observer graph to enable dependency tracking.

4. **Direct Relationship**: Sets up a direct observer relationship where `nodeA` observes `nodeB`.

5. **Transitive Relationship**: Creates a transitive relationship where `nodeB` observes `nodeC`, making `nodeA` indirectly observe `nodeC`.

6. **Observer Storage**: Uses `NodeSet` to collect and store observer relationships during traversal.

7. **Cache Population**: Triggers cache population by explicitly requesting observer lists for each node.

8. **Pre-Cleanup Stats**: Captures cache statistics before any cleanup occurs.

9. **Cache Cleanup**: Forces cache cleanup to simulate real-world cache behavior.

10. **Post-Cleanup Stats**: Captures cache statistics after cleanup to measure cache efficiency.

11. **Resource Cleanup**: Properly closes all nodes to release resources.

12. **Result Snapshot**: Returns a structured snapshot containing cache metrics before/after cleanup and post-cleanup hit ratio.

This implementation comprehensively tests the observer cache behavior under different relationship scenarios while following proper resource management practices.</answer>

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ResetBehaviorSnapshot {
    int beforeResetValue;
    int afterResetValue;
    bool safetyEnabledDuringReset;
};

ResetBehaviorSnapshot test_react_reset_behavior(int initialValue, int multiplier) {
    // 1. Get thread manager instance
    reaction::ThreadManager& threadManager = reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance();
    
    // 2. Reset thread manager state
    threadManager.resetForTesting();
    
    // 3. Create a reactive variable
    auto sourceVar = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 4. Create a calculation
    auto calculation = reaction::calc([&sourceVar, multiplier]() {
        return sourceVar.get() * multiplier;
    });
    
    // 5. Get value before reset
    int beforeResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // 6. Enable thread safety
    threadManager.enableThreadSafety();
    
    // 7. Check if thread safety is enabled
    bool safetyEnabled = threadManager.isThreadSafetyEnabled();
    
    // 8. Reset calculation logic
    calculation.reset([]() { return 42; }); // Reset to constant function
    
    // 9. Update source variable
    sourceVar.value(100);
    
    // 10. Get value after reset
    int afterResetValue = calculation.get();
    
    // 11. Return reset behavior snapshot
    return ResetBehaviorSnapshot{
        beforeResetValue,
        afterResetValue,
        safetyEnabled
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Gets the thread manager instance using `reaction::ThreadManager::getInstance()`
2. Resets the thread manager state using `resetForTesting()`
3. Creates a reactive variable using `reaction::var(initialValue)`
4. Creates a calculation using `reaction::calc()` with a lambda that multiplies the source variable by the multiplier
5. Gets the value before reset using `calculation.get()`
6. Enables thread safety using `enableThreadSafety()`
7. Checks thread safety status using `isThreadSafetyEnabled()`
8. Resets the calculation logic using `reset()` with a constant function
9. Updates the source variable using `value(100)`
10. Gets the value after reset using `calculation.get()`
11. Returns a `ResetBehaviorSnapshot` struct containing all required values

The implementation demonstrates proper usage of the reaction library's reactive programming features including variable creation, calculation dependencies, thread safety management, and dynamic behavior modification through reset operations.</answer>

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

struct ValiditySnapshot {
    bool firstValidBeforeClose;
    bool firstValidAfterClose;
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose;
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate React::operator bool and operator== for validity tracking.
 *
 * @param initialValue Initial value stored inside the reactive variable
 * @return ValiditySnapshot Snapshot describing validity/equality before and after close
 */
inline ValiditySnapshot test_react_validity(int initialValue) {
    // 1. Create reactive variable
    auto source = reaction::var(initialValue);
    
    // 2. Create copy of source
    auto copy = source;
    
    // 3. Check validity before close
    bool firstValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidBeforeClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 4. Check equality before close
    bool copiesEqualBeforeClose = (source == copy);
    
    // 5. Close source node
    source.close();
    
    // 6. Check validity after close
    bool firstValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(source);
    bool copyValidAfterClose = static_cast<bool>(copy);
    
    // 7. Check equality after close with exception handling
    bool copiesEqualAfterClose = false;
    bool equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = false;
    
    try {
        copiesEqualAfterClose = (source == copy);
    } catch (...) {
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose = true;
    }
    
    // 8. Return validity snapshot
    return ValiditySnapshot{
        firstValidBeforeClose,
        firstValidAfterClose,
        copiesEqualBeforeClose,
        copiesEqualAfterClose,
        equalityCheckFailedAfterClose
    };
}

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>

namespace test_reaction {

class TrackingFieldNode : public reaction::ObserverNode {
public:
    explicit TrackingFieldNode(std::string name) : m_name(name) {}
    void valueChanged(bool changed = true) override {
        m_notifications++;
    }
    int notifications() const { return m_notifications; }
    void clear() { m_notifications = 0; }

private:
    std::string m_name;
    int m_notifications = 0;
};

struct FieldGraphCleanupResult {
    bool notifiedBeforeDelete;
    bool notifiedAfterDelete;
};

inline FieldGraphCleanupResult test_field_graph_cleanup(uint64_t objectId) {
    // 1. Create owner and field TrackingFieldNode instances
    TrackingFieldNode owner("owner");
    TrackingFieldNode field("field");
    
    // 2. Get observer graph instance
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 3. Register nodes
    observerGraph.addNode(&owner);
    observerGraph.addNode(&field);
    
    // 4. Get field graph instance
    auto fieldGraph = reaction::FieldGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Register field nodes under object ID
    fieldGraph.addObj(objectId, &field);
    
    // 6. Bind field to owner
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 7. Trigger notification on field
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 8. Check if owner was notified before delete
    bool notifiedBefore = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 9. Clear owner notification count
    owner.clear();
    
    // 10. Clear dependencies on owner
    observerGraph.closeNode(&owner);
    
    // 11. Remove field mappings for object ID
    fieldGraph.deleteObj(objectId);
    
    // 12. Attempt to bind field again
    fieldGraph.bindField(objectId, &field, &owner);
    
    // 13. Trigger notification on field again
    field.valueChanged();
    
    // 14. Check if owner was notified after delete
    bool notifiedAfter = owner.notifications() > 0;
    
    // 15. Close nodes
    observerGraph.closeNode(&field);
    
    // 16. Return field graph cleanup result
    return FieldGraphCleanupResult{notifiedBefore, notifiedAfter};
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>

#pragma once

#include <reaction/core/resource.h>
#include <reaction/graph/observer_graph.h>
#include <memory>
#include <string>
#include <type_traits>

namespace test_reaction {

struct VoidResourceSnapshot {
    bool retrievedIsVoid;
    bool usesSboStorage;
    bool nameAssigned;
    bool nameClearedAfterClose;
};

inline VoidResourceSnapshot test_resource_void_behavior(const std::string &nodeName) {
    // 1. Create shared Resource<Void> instance
    auto resource = std::make_shared<reaction::Resource<Void>>();
    
    // 2. Check if resource uses SBO storage
    bool usesSboStorage = resource->isUsingSBO();
    
    // 3. Get value from resource and check if it's Void type
    auto value = resource->getValue();
    bool retrievedIsVoid = std::is_same_v<decltype(value), reaction::Void>;
    
    // 4. Get observer graph instance
    auto observerGraph = reaction::ObserverGraph::getInstance();
    
    // 5. Register resource node
    observerGraph->addNode(resource);
    
    // 6. Assign node name
    observerGraph->setName(resource, nodeName);
    
    // 7. Check if name was assigned
    std::string assignedName = observerGraph->getName(resource);
    bool nameAssigned = (assignedName == nodeName);
    
    // 8. Close node
    resource->close();
    
    // 9. Check if name was cleared after close
    std::string nameAfterClose = observerGraph->getName(resource);
    bool nameClearedAfterClose = (nameAfterClose.empty());
    
    // 10. Return void resource snapshot
    return VoidResourceSnapshot{
        .retrievedIsVoid = retrievedIsVoid,
        .usesSboStorage = usesSboStorage,
        .nameAssigned = nameAssigned,
        .nameClearedAfterClose = nameClearedAfterClose
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

struct AtomicCompoundSnapshot {
    int afterAddition;
    int afterMultiplication;
    int afterBitwiseAnd;
    int afterShift;
    int postIncrementReturn;
    int finalValue;
    int calcEvaluations;
};

/**
 * Function: Demonstrate atomic compound assignments on a reactive counter.
 *
 * @return AtomicCompoundSnapshot Snapshot with values after each compound operation.
 */
inline AtomicCompoundSnapshot test_atomic_compound_assign() {
    // 1. Create reactive variable initialized to 1
    auto reactiveVar = reaction::var(1);
    
    // 2. Create counter to track calculation evaluations, starting at 0
    auto counter = reaction::var(0);
    
    // 3. Create calculation that depends on reactiveVar and increments counter
    auto calc = reaction::calc([&]() {
        counter += 1;
        return reactiveVar.get();
    });
    
    // Initialize the calculation by getting its value
    calc.get();
    
    // 4. Apply atomic addition: += 5
    reactiveVar += 5;
    
    // 5. Get value after addition
    int afterAddition = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 6. Apply atomic multiplication: *= 3
    reactiveVar *= 3;
    
    // 7. Get value after multiplication
    int afterMultiplication = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 8. Apply atomic bitwise AND: &= 0x0F
    reactiveVar &= 0x0F;
    
    // 9. Get value after bitwise AND
    int afterBitwiseAnd = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 10. Apply atomic left shift: <<= 2
    reactiveVar <<= 2;
    
    // 11. Get value after shift
    int afterShift = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 12. Apply post-increment: v++
    int postIncrementReturn = reactiveVar++;
    
    // 13. Get final value
    int finalValue = reactiveVar.get();
    
    // 14. Get atomic compound snapshot
    int calcEvaluations = counter.get();
    
    return AtomicCompoundSnapshot{
        afterAddition,
        afterMultiplication,
        afterBitwiseAnd,
        afterShift,
        postIncrementReturn,
        finalValue,
        calcEvaluations
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Creates a reactive variable using `reaction::var(1)`
2. Creates a counter using `reaction::var(0)` to track calculation evaluations
3. Creates a calculation using `reaction::calc` that increments the counter and returns the reactive variable's value
4. Applies atomic addition using `operator+=` with value 5
5. Gets the value after addition using `get()`
6. Applies atomic multiplication using `operator*=` with value 3
7. Gets the value after multiplication
8. Applies atomic bitwise AND using `operator&=` with mask 0x0F
9. Gets the value after bitwise AND
10. Applies atomic left shift using `operator<<=` with shift amount 2
11. Gets the value after shift
12. Applies post-increment using `operator++(int)`
13. Gets the final value after post-increment
14. Returns the complete atomic compound snapshot with all required values

The calculation ensures that the counter is incremented each time the reactive variable changes, allowing us to track how many times the calculation was evaluated during the compound operations.</answer>

output.reasoning:
<think>We are going to implement the function step by step as per the specifications.
 Steps:
 1. Create reactive variables for integers a and b using `reaction::var`.
 2. Build a negated sum expression: - (a + b). We can use the operator- on the expression (a+b).
 3. Create a reactive boolean variable (say, flag) and initialize it to false.
 4. Build a logical NOT expression: !flag.
 5. Create a reactive integer variable (say, c) and initialize it to some value (e.g., 0).
 6. Build a bitwise NOT expression: ~c.
 7. Get the initial value of the negated sum expression using `get()`.
 8. Update the variables a, b, and c to new values using `value()` method to trigger recomputation.
 9. Get the updated values of the expressions.
10. Return a UnaryExpressionSnapshot struct with the initial and updated values of the negated sum, the logical NOT after flipping the boolean, and the bitwise NOT.

 Note: We are to use the reaction library as provided.

 Let's break down the code accordingly.

output.text:
<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/cache/graph_cache.h>
#include <reaction/core/observer_node.h>
#include <memory>

namespace test_reaction {

struct NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot {
    std::size_t entriesBefore;
    std::size_t entriesAfterCache;
    std::size_t entriesAfterInvalidate;
    std::size_t hitCount;
    std::size_t missCount;
    double hitRatio;
    bool metricsCachedExists;
    bool metricsClearedAfterInvalidate;
};

NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot test_node_metrics_cache() {
    // 1. Create NodeMetricsCache instance
    auto cache = std::make_shared<reaction::NodeMetricsCache>();
    
    // 2. Create ObserverNode instance
    auto node = std::make_shared<reaction::ObserverNode>();
    
    // 3. Get cache statistics before caching
    auto statsBefore = cache->getStats();
    
    // 4. Query metrics before caching
    const auto* metricsBefore = cache->getCachedNodeMetrics(node);
    
    // 5. Cache node metrics
    reaction::NodeMetrics dummyMetrics{true, 0, 0, 0};
    cache->cacheNodeMetrics(node, dummyMetrics);
    
    // 6. Query metrics after caching
    const auto* metricsAfter = cache->getCachedNodeMetrics(node);
    
    // 7. Get cache statistics after caching
    auto statsAfter = cache->getStats();
    
    // 8. Invalidate all entries
    cache->invalidateAllEntries();
    
    // 9. Query metrics after invalidation
    const auto* metricsAfterInvalid = cache->getCachedNodeMetrics(node);
    
    // 10. Get cache statistics after invalidation
    auto statsAfterInvalid = cache->getStats();
    
    // 11. Return node metrics cache snapshot
    return NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot{
        .entriesBefore = statsBefore.entries,
        .entriesAfterCache = statsAfter.entries,
        .entriesAfterInvalidate = statsAfterInvalid.entries,
        .hitCount = statsAfterInvalid.hitCount,
        .missCount = statsAfterInvalid.missCount,
        .hitRatio = statsAfterInvalid.hitRatio,
        .metricsCachedExists = (metricsAfter != nullptr),
        .metricsClearedAfterInvalidate = (metricsAfterInvalid == nullptr)
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

This implementation follows all the specified requirements:

1. Creates a `NodeMetricsCache` instance using `std::make_shared`
2. Creates an `ObserverNode` instance using `std::make_shared`
3. Gets cache statistics before any caching operations
4. Queries metrics before caching (should return nullptr)
5. Caches node metrics with dummy data
6. Queries metrics after caching (should return valid metrics)
7. Gets cache statistics after caching
8. Invalidates all cache entries
9. Queries metrics after invalidation (should return nullptr)
10. Gets final cache statistics
11. Returns a populated `NodeMetricsCacheSnapshot` struct with all required fields

The implementation uses the correct functions from the `reaction` library as specified in the reference materials.</answer>

namespace test_reaction {

/**

struct ForceRegisterSnapshot {

    bool safetyAfterMainForce;
    bool safetyAfterWorkerForce;

/**

}  // namespace test_reaction

```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/reaction.h>
#include <reaction/expression/expression_builders.h>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: RawExpressionSnapshot
 *
 * Captures the values produced by raw expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) before and after updating
 * their reactive inputs.
 */
struct RawExpressionSnapshot {
    int initialSum;
    int initialNegatedSum;
    int initialChainedValue;

    int updatedSum;
    int updatedNegatedSum;
    int updatedChainedValue;
};

/**
 * Function: test_raw_expression_builders
 *
 * Demonstrates how low-level expression builder helpers
 * (`make_binary_expr`, `make_unary_expr`) can be combined with
 * `expr` to build reactive calculations without using the higher
 * level `make_binary_op` / `make_unary_op` shortcuts.
 *
 * The function:
 * - Creates three reactive integers A, B, C.
 * - Builds sum = A + B via make_binary_expr.
 * - Builds negated = -(A + B) via make_unary_expr(make_binary_expr(...)).
 * - Builds chained = -(A + B) * C via nested make_* calls.
 * - Updates A and C and records all expression values before and after.
 *
 * @param a Initial value for the first input
 * @param b Initial value for the second input
 * @param c Initial value for the chaining multiplier
 * @return RawExpressionSnapshot
 *         Snapshot of sum / negated / chained values before and after updates.
 */
inline RawExpressionSnapshot test_raw_expression_builders(int a, int b, int c);

}  // namespace test_reaction
```

<answer>```cpp
#pragma once

#include <reaction/expression/atomic_operations.h>
#include <cstdint>

namespace test_reaction {

/**
 * Struct: AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *
 * Records the value of an integer after a sequence of atomic compound helper
 * operations and whether each operation reported a change.
 */
struct AtomicHelperSnapshot {
    int32_t finalValue;
    bool addReportedChange;
    bool multiplyReportedChange;
    bool andReportedChange;
    bool leftShiftReportedChange;
    bool incrementReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Helper implementation type that mimics the minimal interface required by
 * reaction::atomicCompoundAssign helpers. It stores an integer and exposes
 * an atomicOperation method that receives a lambda and tracks whether the
 * value was changed.
 */
class IntAtomicImpl {
public:
    explicit IntAtomicImpl(int32_t initial) : m_value(initial), m_lastReportedChange(false) {}

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    template <typename F>
    void atomicOperation(F&& op, bool forceNotify) {
        bool changed = op(m_value);
        if (forceNotify) {
            changed = true;
        }
        m_lastReportedChange = changed;
    }

    int32_t value() const { return m_value; }
    bool lastReportedChange() const { return m_lastReportedChange; }

private:
    int32_t m_value;
    bool m_lastReportedChange;
};

/**
 * Function: test_atomic_compound_helpers
 *
 * Demonstrates the low-level atomic compound helper APIs:
 * - atomicCompoundAssign
 * - atomicAddAssign
 * - atomicMultiplyAssign
 * - atomicBitwiseAndAssign
 * - atomicLeftShiftAssign
 * - atomicIncrement
 *
 * These helpers operate on a simple IntAtomicImpl type instead of a full
 * React wrapper, making it easy to observe value and change reporting.
 *
 * @param initial Initial integer value
 * @return AtomicHelperSnapshot
 *         Snapshot containing the final value and which operations reported
 *         that a change occurred.
 */
inline AtomicHelperSnapshot test_atomic_compound_helpers(int32_t initial) {
    IntAtomicImpl impl(initial);
    
    // Reset change flags before testing
    impl.atomicOperation([](int32_t&) { return false; });
    
    // Test atomic add assignment
    reaction::atomicAddAssign(impl, 5);
    bool addChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic multiply assignment
    reaction::atomicMultiplyAssign(impl, 3);
    bool multiplyChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic bitwise AND assignment
    reaction::atomicBitwiseAndAssign(impl, 0x0F);
    bool andChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic left shift assignment
    reaction::atomicLeftShiftAssign(impl, 2);
    bool leftShiftChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Test atomic increment
    reaction::atomicIncrement(impl);
    bool incrementChanged = impl.lastReportedChange();
    
    // Get final value
    int32_t finalVal = impl.value();
    
    return AtomicHelperSnapshot{
        finalVal,
        addChanged,
        multiplyChanged,
        andChanged,
        leftShiftChanged,
        incrementChanged
    };
}

}  // namespace test_reaction
```</answer>
